questions.scenario: A team wants to move the normal checkout API from Container Apps to a VM because they understand SSH better than revisions. What is the best review question?

options.text: What specific OS-level control does the API need, and is that control worth patching, disk, process, and access ownership?
Can we make the VM name match the container app name?
Can we skip health checks after moving to a VM?
Can we turn off managed identity because SSH is available?

questions.explanation: Comfort with SSH is not the same as a workload requirement. A VM should earn its extra ownership.

questions.scenario: The platform team is reviewing old Azure VMs and wants to decide which machines still have a real reason to exist. They need evidence that separates necessary control from historical accident. Which evidence helps decide whether a VM should remain a VM?

options.text: The workload needs a specific OS package, kernel behavior, local agent, or disk shape.
The team has a tested patching and recovery process.
The machine exists because nobody has revisited it.
Logs, metrics, and restart behavior are understood by more than one person.

questions.explanation: A VM can be appropriate when the requirement and operating plan are clear. Historical accident is not enough.
